title: Dev fee is double-counted, minting reflected balance to the contract on every taxed transfer
claim: The constructor enables an 8% team/dev fee, but `_getRValues()` subtracts only the reflection fee from the recipient's reflected amount and never subtracts the dev fee. `_takeCharity()` still credits the full dev fee to `address(this)`, so each taxed transfer creates extra reflected balance for the contract instead of sourcing those tokens from the sender's transfer amount.
impact: The contract accumulates synthetic tokens that were never actually debited from the transfer, then sells them for real ETH through the auto-swap path and forwards proceeds to the team wallet. This breaks token accounting and can continuously extract value from the AMM and holders.
paths: Any buy after trading opens where fees are active
Any sell where fees are active
Any wallet-to-wallet transfer between non-fee-exempt addresses while `_teamDev` remains nonzero

title: Cooldown is keyed by `sender`, enabling a market-wide 60 second buy denial of service
claim: The cooldown updates `_lastTx[sender]`. On buys, the token transfer is from `uniswapV2Pair` to the buyer, so the first successful buy sets `_lastTx[uniswapV2Pair] = block.timestamp + 60 seconds`. Every later buy during that window reverts, regardless of buyer.
impact: Any attacker can keep the market's buy side frozen indefinitely with a dust buy roughly once per minute, monopolizing access and creating a permissionless market-wide denial of service.
paths: Buyer A purchases from the pair, setting cooldown on `uniswapV2Pair`
Buyer B attempts another buy within 60 seconds and hits the cooldown revert
An attacker repeats small buys to keep all other buys blocked

title: Owner can blacklist the LP pair and freeze all trading
claim: `RemoveSniper()` forbids blacklisting only the router, not `uniswapV2Pair`. If the owner blacklists the pair, sells revert because `recipient == uniswapV2Pair`, and buys revert because the token transfer is called by the pair so `msg.sender == uniswapV2Pair`.
impact: The owner has an immediate hard-stop lever over the market and can trap liquidity and holders by permanently freezing both buys and sells.
paths: Any sell reverts because the recipient pair is blacklisted
Any buy reverts because `msg.sender` is the blacklisted pair

title: Ownership can be made to look renounced and later reclaimed
claim: `lock()` sets the owner to the zero address but stores the former owner in `_previousOwner`, and `unlock()` later restores full ownership to that stored address after the lock period. This creates a hidden reclaim path that can make the contract appear ownerless without actually removing admin control.
impact: Users can be misled into trusting the token as renounced while the same controller retains the ability to later recover privileged functions such as blacklist management, swap controls, and fee-wallet changes.
paths: Users treat the contract as renounced or ownerless
After `_lockTime`, the previous owner calls `unlock()` and regains all admin powers

title: Forced full-balance auto-swaps use `amountOutMin = 0`, making them trivially sandwichable
claim: When the contract's token balance reaches the threshold, the next non-buy transfer triggers a market sale of the contract's entire token balance. The swap uses `swapExactTokensForETHSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens(..., amountOutMin = 0, ...)`, so it accepts any execution price.
impact: MEV searchers can predict and sandwich these forced sells, worsening execution for the contract and extracting value from fee proceeds and the user transaction that triggers the swap.
paths: Observe a non-buy transfer in the mempool
Front-run to push price down, let the contract sell at any price, then back-run the recovery

title: A reverting or gas-heavy team wallet can turn auto-swap into a sell-side denial of service
claim: The owner can arbitrarily change `_teamDevAddress`, and the transfer path forwards ETH with Solidity `transfer()`, which reverts if the recipient contract rejects ETH or needs more than 2300 gas. Once auto-swap is triggered, that reverting ETH send reverts the whole token transfer.
impact: After the contract reaches the swap threshold, sells and normal transfers can become impossible until the owner intervenes, creating a realistic buy-only or frozen-market condition.
paths: Owner sets `_teamDevAddress` to a contract that reverts on receiving ETH or needs more than 2300 gas
The contract accumulates enough tokens to trigger auto-swap
Any non-buy transfer hits `sendETHToTeamDev()` and reverts
